main.label: Main Page
main.location1: The University of Tokyo, Hongo Campus

concept.title: Concept
concept.body:
As the gravity of “rightness” fades in the modern age, |we drift freely
Like struggling slowly through water against resistance,
or wandering in that moment before waking from a dream, |unsure of where we are
there is both comfort and constraint in drifting
Given endless choices, |we search for the correct answer |and become averaged out in the process

What should we rely on, |what should we seek, |and where should we go?
To contemplate “drifting”

about.title: About
about.body:
The UTokyo iii Exhibition is an exhibition exploring the fusion of technology and art.
Supervised by faculty in the field of information and media art, the exhibition is planned, produced, and operated primarily by students of the Graduate School of Interdisciplinary Information Studies at the University of Tokyo, along with students from a diverse range of disciplines.
about.registration:
Registration is required for entry.
Walk-in registration is available on the day, but pre-registration is recommended as crowds are expected.
